derivation: MAC address = 24-bit IEEE-assigned OUI + 24-bit vendor-assigned serial, so the 48-bit address is globally unique, not merely locally unique.
Ripple-carry delay is O(n) because each full adder waits for the previous carry; carry-look-ahead computes carries in parallel from G/P terms, so it is faster.

Only options are TCP and UDP; neither transport provides security. The correct answer to 'connect securely across a public network' is a VPN / encrypted tunnel, which is absent, so no option is truly correct. A is only least-wrong because TLS runs over TCP. Option list is truncated.
Handling growth in data volume/load is scalability; extensibility is the ability to add new features or capability, not more data.
1 TB = 10^12 bytes = one trillion bytes; 'flash' and 'storage' name a medium/category, not a quantity.
Data bus width 32 bits / 8 bits per byte = 4 bytes transferred per memory access.
Full mesh gives every host a direct link to every other (n(n-1)/2 links), so multiple alternate paths survive any single failure; ring gives 2 paths, star has a single hub SPOF.

Whether purchased software runs at all is set by the platform's system requirements (CPU/OS/RAM/disk), so hardware compatibility is the binding constraint; 'expensive' is not a correctness criterion and D is a compound trap.

Stem is garbled but recoverable ('why is IP used rather than the Ethernet address'): IP addresses are hierarchical (network prefix + host) so routers can aggregate and forward on the prefix; MAC addresses are flat and carry no location information.

A receive equalizer is a transversal filter built from a tapped delay line: delayed copies are weighted and summed to cancel intersymbol interference from delay distortion. Gearshift, descrambler and difference engine are unrelated.
Page traffic is the rate of page-ins and page-outs, i.e. movement of pages between memory and backing store (excessive page traffic = thrashing). D describes the resident set, a static count.

Five-state model = new, ready, running, blocked/waiting, terminated. Ready, Terminated and Blocked are all fundamental states; 'Executed' (past tense) is not a state, the state is 'running/executing'. Independently derived B, matching the panel's correction of the crowd-sourced key.
LRU = Least Recently Used; the other three expansions are invented.
The IEEE 802.11 WLAN family, including 802.11a, is branded Wi-Fi. Ethernet is 802.3, WiMAX is 802.16, and 802.11g is a different PHY (2.4 GHz) not another name for 802.11a (5 GHz).

The kernel is kept memory-resident because it is the most frequently invoked code (every system call, interrupt and context switch); paging it out would fault on nearly every dispatch. Managing interrupt calls is a function the kernel performs, not the reason it stays in primary memory.
